front: Poem: 'Stone walls do not a prison make, / Nor iron bars a cage'; speaker sings of 'the glories of my King'
Poem: 'fishes that tipple in the deep' and 'birds that wanton in the air' 'know no such liberty' as the imprisoned speaker
Poem: ends 'I could not love thee, Dear, so much, / Lov'd I not Honour more'; speaker leaves for battle
back: To Lucasta, Going to the Wars (Richard Lovelace)
work: To Lucasta, Going to the Wars

front: Poem: Lucasta based on Lucy Sacheverell, nicknamed 'Lux Casta' meaning 'pure light'
back: To Lucasta, Going to the Wars (Richard Lovelace)
work: To Lucasta, Going to the Wars

front: Poem: 'The Grasshopper' dedicated to 'Noble Friend' Charles Cotton, depicting an insect getting drunk on an 'oaten beard'
